etymology.description: Depicts a kneeling figure turning around with mouth open -> original form ใ’ซ -> stylized as ๆฌ (ๆฌ ) (qiร n, "yawn") showing a front-facing mouth
etymology.interpretation: The character ๆ—ก(jรฌ), a pictogram, represents a kneeling figure turning away with their mouth open, emphasizing the act of pausing or ceasing, akin to stopping speech or action. This depiction connects to the idea of turning away or abstaining from further action. Its structure is related to ๆฌ (ๆฌ ) (qiร n, "yawn"), which also features a figure with an open mouth, although facing forward, suggesting a common theme of cessation or incompletion.
